开.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 門 (door) + 一 (latch/bar) + 廾 (pair of hands) depict opening a latched door.

但.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 亻 (person) + phonetic 旦, meaning 'only; but'.

因.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 囗 (enclosure/mat) + 大 (person) depicts a person lying on a mat; original form of 茵.

只.wiki_summary: Wiktionary entry for traditional 隻: ideogrammic compound of 隹 (short-tailed bird) + 又 (hand), depicting one bird in hand, contrasting with 雙 (two birds).
只.difference: Our data classifies 隻 as simple_indicative, but Wiktionary classifies it as a compound_ideograph (隹 + 又).

从.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies traditional 從 as a phono-semantic compound: semantic 辵 (walk/follow) + phonetic 从.
从.difference: Our data classifies 從 as compound_ideograph (two people following), but Wiktionary's primary classification is phono_semantic_compound (semantic 辵 + phonetic 从).

想.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 心 (heart/mind) + phonetic 相, meaning to think or consider.

实.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 宀 (roof) + components yielding 貫 (string of cowries/wealth), originally meaning 'full' or 'fruit'.

军.wiki_summary: Wiktionary's primary classification of 軍 is phono-semantic compound (semantic 車 chariot + phonetic element); the Shuowen Jiezi offers an alternative ideogrammic reading (cart + surround).
军.difference: Our data classifies 軍 as compound_ideograph, but Wiktionary's primary classification is phono_semantic_compound, with the ideogrammic reading only as a secondary Shuowen alternative.

者.wiki_summary: Pictogram originally depicting a sugarcane plant with stems and leaves, later phonetically borrowed for the nominalizing suffix meaning 'one who'.
者.difference: Our data classifies 者 as simple_indicative, but Wiktionary classifies it as a pictograph (象形).
